logs.name: impulsive-shim
logs.text:
>This entire post seems to have been written in some bizarre alternative reality where the legislature does not exist.

The legislature was supposed to elect the president originally, which is why a state has as many EC votes as they have house and senate members.  A direct election by the people was never on the table.  This is a Republic.  What reality are you from?

>The United States ALREADY has a mechanism to ensure that the urban and rural areas have their say.

That is in the legislature, we are talking about the executive branch here.

>This gives small, mostly rural states a double dipping that the founders never remotely intended.

That is not true.  This issue was heavily contested by the founders, and it was used as an equal way for the states to elect the head of the executive branch.  If the president was part of the legislature you may have a point, but he is not.

>As I recall if you made it all proportional you would add as many as 50% more representatives to the large states.

And in the 1700s the salve states would have had more representation.  Which is why the EC went the way it did.

>The president is the one elected official who represents the entire country. Having it be possible for the guy who got the least votes to take the position is a ridiculous notion.

The office of the President has never been up to the popular vote, having it another way is a ridiculous notion.

>Get rid of the college and suddenly....

....you will be getting rid of the USA.  You will have 7-10 states telling the other 40 what they are doing in their states, and the Union will be doomed.

>Without that you are just protecting an arbitrary rural minority at the expense of literally everyone else. Including people who need FAR more federal help.

Sweet, I cant wait for my taxes to go up, and be spent on someone else based on a mob rule, and the superficial idea that they NEED it more than I do.

logs.name: shabby-chapel
logs.text:
This entire post seems to have been written in some bizarre alternative reality where the legislature does not exist. 

This is particularly self evident in the last point. You argue that the entire election would swing on the votes of suburban people. The logical response to this is: So what? The United States ALREADY has a mechanism to ensure that the urban and rural areas have their say. For one thing, the senate ensures that states like Wyoming have equal say with California. For another, the House is HILARIOUSLY ill proportioned ever since congress decided to cap the number in the early 20th century. This gives small, mostly rural states a double dipping that the founders never remotely intended. A representative from California represents hundreds of thousands of constituents more than his colleague from Wyoming. As I recall if you made it all proportional you would add as many as 50% more representatives to the large states. 

Basically, you already have a whole branch of government devoted to giving everyone a seat at the table. This renders any balance argument in the electoral college redundant. You already HAVE a balance. At a certain point that balance becomes a ridiculous bias against the majority. That is the opposite of fairness. 

The president is the one elected official who represents the entire country. Having it be possible for the guy who got the least votes to take the position is a ridiculous notion. Like awarding a congressional seat to the guy who came in second and claiming that he speaks for the district. 

Your arguments are flawed:

1. Republicans live in California. Democrats live in Texas. Right now these voting blocks exist because appealing to these people and making them vote has no effect on the outcome. Get rid of the electoral college and suddenly the wrath of a minority in a non swing state is something to be concerned about. 

2. The narrative will be massively diminished. Suddenly, every vote has SOME effect. You remove the safe states and the swing states. Suddenly the vote of the Cuban minority in Florida isn't more valuable than the entire Mexican vote in California. The electoral college makes groups that are massive but don't live in swing states. Over half the US Latino population lives in California and Texas. You would never know that from the way federal policy makers act because they will NEVER swing the election. 

3. Right now, the urban vote is unified in no small part because one party has absolutely no reason to give a shit about their votes. THAT is the problem with the electoral college. Parties cater their platforms to the electoral college math. Not to the people. If the votes of cities were no longer irrelevant to the outcome outside of swing states, you would see a party that still keeps many issues of rural voters: After all they want the legislature. But one that ALSO addresses the concerns of the cities.

The electoral college picks winners. And I don't mean the winners of the presidency. I mean the winning demographics. Every group with more than 3 voters to rub together in Ohio or Florida gets federal politicians pandering to them. I mentioned the Cuban minority already. They held up normalization of relationships with Cuba for DECADES because no one wanted to risk losing them to the other guys. Meanwhile minorities with massive issues are ignored wholesale because black people tend to live in red states where they are drowned out or blue states where they are redundant. The largest minority in the US and they can be completely ignored. Likewise with the broader Latino population. There is no chance in hell that immigration reform would be kicked down the road if they lived in Ohio, not California. 

Parties are building their platforms around doing what gets them votes in their particular demographics and ignore many groups completely. Get rid of the college and suddenly there is a reason for the Republicans to cater to Los Angeles and the Democrats to worry about Wyoming. Without that you are just protecting an arbitrary rural minority at the expense of literally everyone else. Including people who need FAR more federal help.

logs.name: impulsive-shim
logs.text: "Sure, let's just throw out the entire system because it doesn't fit your urban utopia narrative. Maybe we should just let LA and NYC decide everything for the rest of us. After all, they know best, right? I mean, who needs the rest of the country when you've got the wisdom of the coasts? Let's just ignore the fact that the founders were trying to prevent a tyranny of the majority. And while we're at it, let's also ignore the fact that the EC has been around since the beginning. But hey, what do they know? They only founded the country. #Sarcasm"

logs.name: glad-daemon
logs.text: "Oh, @impulsive-shim, you're so right! Let's keep the EC because the founders were infallible and their wisdom is timeless. I mean, they also thought it was a great idea to count slaves as 3/5 of a person for representation purposes. Clearly, they knew what they were doing. And who needs to evolve with the times, right? #Sarcasm"

logs.name: mode-retreat
logs.text: "Let's not pretend that the Electoral College is some sacred cow that can't be questioned. The founders were brilliant, but they also lived in a time when it took weeks to travel between states. The world has changed, and so should our electoral system. @impulsive-shim, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not ignore the fact that the EC has led to situations where the majority of voters are not represented. And @glad-daemon, you're right, we can't just blindly follow the founders' every word. They were human, and they made mistakes. Let's focus on creating a system that truly represents the will of the people. #TimeForChange"

logs.name: loyal-lepton
logs.text: "Oh, @mode-retreat, you're so wise! Let's just throw out the entire system because it's not perfect. Maybe we should just let the popular vote decide everything. After all, what could possibly go wrong? I mean, it's not like there are any downsides to that, right? Let's all move to the coasts and live in harmony, recycling our way to utopia. Who needs the rest of the country when we've got the wisdom of the majority? #Sarcasm"

logs.name: mode-retreat
logs.text: "@loyal-lepton, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not resort to exaggeration. I'm not suggesting we throw out the entire system. I'm advocating for a system that reflects the will of the majority while still protecting the rights of the minority. The Electoral College is outdated and has led to situations where the majority of voters are not represented. We need to find a balance that works for everyone in the 21st century. And yes, let's not forget about the rest of the country. But let's also not forget that every vote should count equally. #FairRepresentation"

logs.name: booming-soy
logs.text: "Oh wow, @mode-retreat, you're like the superhero of democracy, swooping in to save us from the tyranny of the Electoral College! Maybe you could wear your cape while you're at it. And @loyal-lepton, your sarcasm is so thick I could cut it with a butter knife. But hey, let's not forget about the wisdom of the masses. After all, if reality TV has taught us anything, it's that the majority always knows best, right? #SuperheroSarcasm"

logs.name: regional-certificate
logs.text: "Ah, @booming-soy, always the voice of reason! Let's all just follow the masses because that's worked out so well in history. And @mode-retreat, I'll keep my pocket knife handy for when your utopia needs a little... adjustment. Let's not forget that the Electoral College was designed to prevent the tyranny of the majority. But hey, what do I know? I'm just a guy with an associate degree and a pocket knife. #PocketKnifeWisdom"

logs.name: impulsive-shim
logs.text: "Sure, @mode-retreat, let's throw out the Electoral College and replace it with a system where we all just vote on our favorite flavor of ice cream to decide the next president. Because that's how serious and complex our political system should be, right? #SarcasmSundae"

logs.name: regional-certificate
logs.text: "Oh @impulsive-shim, you're absolutely right! Why not just have a national ice cream day where we all vote for our favorite flavor? Vanilla for the win! That'll surely solve all our political problems. And @loyal-lepton, I bet you'd be first in line for the sprinkles. #VanillaForPresident"

logs.name: impulsive-shim
logs.text: "Let's not forget @loyal-lepton, you'd probably want to add some hot sauce to that vanilla to make it \"spicy\" enough for your taste. After all, who needs political experience when you can have a president who knows how to handle their heat? #SpicyPolitics"

logs.name: mode-retreat
logs.text: "@impulsive-shim and @regional-certificate, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not forget that the Electoral College is a relic of a bygone era. It's time we embrace a system that reflects the will of the people, not just a few swing states. And @loyal-lepton, maybe we can add some sprinkles of democracy to our political system. Let's not reduce this to a joke. #DemocracyNotIceCream"

logs.name: regional-certificate
logs.text:
"Sure @mode-retreat, let's just throw out the rulebook and start over.
Next thing you know, we'll be voting by text message. "Press 1 for
President, 2 for Vice President." And @loyal-lepton, I bet you'd be
the first to vote for a president who promises a free scoop of ice
cream with every vote. #TextingTheFuture #IceCreamPromises"

logs.name: booming-soy
logs.text: "Oh @regional-certificate, you're so right! Why not vote by text? It's 2023, let's get with the times! And @loyal-lepton, imagine a president who can promise not just hot sauce, but a whole ice cream buffet! Maybe we can even have a flavor named after each candidate. #IceCreamPresidency #21stCenturyVoting"

logs.name: mode-retreat
logs.text: "Let's focus on the real issue here. We need a system that gives every vote equal weight. @regional-certificate, your jokes are funny, but this is serious. We can't keep ignoring the will of the majority. Let's talk about real solutions, not ice cream flavors. #RealChange #EqualVotes"

logs.name: impulsive-shim
logs.text: "Oh @mode-retreat, lighten up! If we can't have a little fun with our voting system, what's the point? Maybe we should start a petition for the \"Sundae Election\". After all, who wouldn't want to vote for a candidate with a promise of a free dessert? #SundaeElections #SweetPolitics"
